name: Toam Yum Gai (Thai Soup)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large covered stockpot, heat olive and sesame oil on high heat.
2. Add cloves, bay leaves, thyme (pounded a bit to release flavor), onion, and carrots. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring if desired.
3. Add chicken pieces and pour 1 cup of water over them. Cover and cook for 5 minutes on high.
4. Add the remaining water, cover, and continue heating until it starts to boil. Then reduce heat and simmer covered for 30 minutes. Skim off foam occasionally.
5. Remove chicken, debone, and cut into bite-sized pieces. Strain soup, avoiding sediment at the bottom.
6. In a separate pot, heat 4 cups of stock until it begins to boil, then reduce heat until it is just simmering.
7. Add galanga, lemongrass, and Kaffir lime leaves (pounded to release flavor). Add mushrooms, chicken (and shrimp if needed), fish sauce, and chili paste. Cover and cook for another 5 minutes.
8. Serve with lemon juice, cilantro, and thinly sliced jalapenos or other hot peppers at the table. Fresh liquorice basil also adds a nice touch.
Comments/Modifications:
We used Swanson's reduced salt chicken stock for the water initially due to using boneless skinless chicken. Adding soaked shiitake mushrooms with soaking juice enhanced flavor significantly.
